name: ExtensionAdministrableProductDefinition_ProducedFrom
title: R5: Indicates the specific manufactured items that are part of the 'formOf' product that are used in the preparation of this specific administrable form (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `AdministrableProductDefinition.producedFrom` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`AdministrableProductDefinition.producedFrom` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ManufacturedItemDefinition)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `AdministrableProductDefinition.producedFrom` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ManufacturedItemDefinition)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `AdministrableProductDefinition.producedFrom` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `AdministrableProductDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `AdministrableProductDefinition.producedFrom` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Indicates the specific manufactured items that are part of the 'formOf' product that are used in the preparation of this specific administrable form (new)
element.comment:
Element `AdministrableProductDefinition.producedFrom` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `AdministrableProductDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `AdministrableProductDefinition.producedFrom` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Indicates the specific manufactured items that are part of the 'formOf' product that are used in the preparation of this specific administrable form
element.definition: Indicates the specific manufactured items that are part of the 'formOf' product that are used in the preparation of this specific administrable form.  In some cases, an administrable form might use all of the items from the overall product (or there might only be one item), while in other cases, an administrable form might use only a subset of the items available in the overall product.  For example, an administrable form might involve combining a liquid and a powder available as part of an overall product, but not involve applying the also supplied cream.
